q: Those who can make you believe absurdities can make you commit atrocities.
h:
“Those who can make you believe absurdities can make you commit atrocities. ” —
Voltaire

q: Be brave to stand for what you believe in even if you stand alone.
h:
“Be brave to stand for what you believe in even if you stand alone.” —
Roy T. Bennett

q: The first half of life is devoted to forming a healthy ego, the second half is going inward and letting go of it.
h:
“The first half of life is devoted to forming a healthy ego, the second half is going inward and letting go of it.” —
Carl Jung

q: I've always lived in the moment.
h:
“I've always lived in the moment.” —
Yanni

q: Genius, in truth, means little more than the faculty of perceiving in an unhabitual way.
h:
“Genius, in truth, means little more than the faculty of perceiving in an unhabitual way.” —
William James

q: Our attitude toward life determines life's attitude towards us.
h:
“Our attitude toward life determines life's attitude towards us.” —
Earl Nightingale

q: The path is difficult to ensure worthiness. The lazy look elsewhere, The persevering find riches.
h:
“The path is difficult to ensure worthiness. The lazy look elsewhere, The persevering find riches.” —
Ming-Dao Deng

q: To avoid criticism, do nothing, say nothing, and be nothing.
h:
“To avoid criticism, do nothing, say nothing, and be nothing.” —
Elbert Hubbard

q: A tiny change today brings a dramatically different tomorrow.
h:
“A tiny change today brings a dramatically different tomorrow.” —
Richard Bach

q: Someday death will take us to another star.
h:
“Someday death will take us to another star.” —
Vincent van Gogh

q: Best be yourself, imperial, plain, and true.
h:
“Best be yourself, imperial, plain, and true.” —
Robert Browning

q: Happiness comes when you believe in what you are doing, know what you are doing, and love what you are doing.
h:
“Happiness comes when you believe in what you are doing, know what you are doing, and love what you are doing.” —
Brian Tracy

q: The moment you stop to think about whether you love someone, you've already stopped loving that person forever.
h:
“The moment you stop to think about whether you love someone, you've already stopped loving that person forever.” —
Carlos Ruiz Zafon

q: Man's character is his fate.
h:
“Man's character is his fate.” —
Heraclitus

q: Whatever is begun in anger, ends in shame.
h:
“Whatever is begun in anger, ends in shame.” —
Benjamin Franklin

q: No valid plans for the future can be made by those who have no capacity for living now.
h:
“No valid plans for the future can be made by those who have no capacity for living now.” —
Alan Watts

q: If you want to feel happy, do something for yourself. If you want to feel fulfilled, do something for someone else.
h:
“If you want to feel happy, do something for yourself. If you want to feel fulfilled, do something for someone else.” —
Simon Sinek

q: Relax. Nothing is under control.
h:
“Relax. Nothing is under control.” —
Adi Da Samraj

q: The road to success and the road to failure are almost exactly the same.
h:
“The road to success and the road to failure are almost exactly the same.” —
Colin R. Davis

q: Man must use what he has, not hope for what is not.
h:
“Man must use what he has, not hope for what is not.” —
G.I. Gurdjieff

q: We are willing to believe anything other than the truth.
h:
“We are willing to believe anything other than the truth.” —
Carlos Ruiz Zafon

q: One day, in retrospect, the years of struggle will strike you as the most beautiful.
h:
“One day, in retrospect, the years of struggle will strike you as the most beautiful.” —
Sigmund Freud

q: The least deviation from truth will be multiplied later.
h:
“The least deviation from truth will be multiplied later.” —
Aristotle

q: The golden opportunity you are seeking is in yourself. It is not in your environment; it is not in luck or chance, or the help of others; it is in yourself alone.
h:
“The golden opportunity you are seeking is in yourself. It is not in your environment; it is not in luck or chance, or the help of others; it is in yourself alone. ” —
Orison Swett Marden

q: Everything you can imagine is real.
h:
“Everything you can imagine is real.” —
Pablo Picasso

q: Do not ignore your gift. Your gift is the thing you do the absolute best with the least amount of effort.
h:
“Do not ignore your gift. Your gift is the thing you do the absolute best with the least amount of effort.” —
Steve Harvey

q: Whatever your mind can conceive and believe, it can achieve.
h:
“Whatever your mind can conceive and believe, it can achieve.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: Faithless is he that says farewell when the road darkens.
h:
“Faithless is he that says farewell when the road darkens.” —
J.R.R. Tolkien

q: Ask no questions, and you'll be told no lies.
h:
“Ask no questions, and you'll be told no lies.” —
Charles Dickens

q: Follow your heart, listen to your inner voice, stop caring about what others think.
h:
“Follow your heart, listen to your inner voice, stop caring about what others think.” —
Roy T. Bennett

q: You can easily judge the character of a man by how he treats those who can do nothing for him.
h:
“You can easily judge the character of a man by how he treats those who can do nothing for him.” —
Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

q: Life goes by very fast. And the worst thing in life that you can have is a job that you hate, and have no energy and creativity in.
h:
“Life goes by very fast. And the worst thing in life that you can have is a job that you hate, and have no energy and creativity in.” —
Robert Greene

q: What the eye doesn't see and the mind doesn't know, doesn't exist.
h:
“What the eye doesn't see and the mind doesn't know, doesn't exist.” —
D. H. Lawrence

q: We build too many walls and not enough bridges.
h:
“We build too many walls and not enough bridges.” —
Isaac Newton

q: Ripe fruit falls by itself - but it doesn't fall in your mouth.
h:
“Ripe fruit falls by itself - but it doesn't fall in your mouth. ” —
Chinese Proverb

q: When you have vision it affects your attitude. Your attitude is optimistic rather than pessimistic.
h:
“When you have vision it affects your attitude. Your attitude is optimistic rather than pessimistic. ” —
Charles Swindoll

q: Success is getting what you want... Happiness is wanting what you get.
h:
“Success is getting what you want... Happiness is wanting what you get.” —
Dale Carnegie

q: Where there's life, there's hope.
h:
“Where there's life, there's hope.” —
Stephen Hawking

q: Bloom where you are planted!
h:
“Bloom where you are planted!” —
Mary Engelbreit

q: Do every act of your life as if it were your last.
h:
“Do every act of your life as if it were your last.” —
Marcus Aurelius

q: The glow of one warm thought is to me worth more than money.
h:
“The glow of one warm thought is to me worth more than money.” —
Thomas Jefferson

q: Human behavior flows from three main sources: desire, emotion, and knowledge.
h:
“Human behavior flows from three main sources: desire, emotion, and knowledge.” —
Plato

q: Keep your eyes on the stars, but remember to keep your feet on the ground.
h:
“Keep your eyes on the stars, but remember to keep your feet on the ground.” —
Theodore Roosevelt

q: Sometimes success isn't about making the right decision, it's more about making some decision.
h:
“Sometimes success isn't about making the right decision, it's more about making some decision.” —
Robin Sharma

q: We are most nearly ourselves when we achieve the seriousness of the child at play.
h:
“We are most nearly ourselves when we achieve the seriousness of the child at play.” —
Heraclitus

q: Hardly anybody recognizes the most significant moments of their life at the time they happen.
h:
“Hardly anybody recognizes the most significant moments of their life at the time they happen.” —
W.P. Kinsella

q: You can have the mind or you can have the moment.
h:
“You can have the mind or you can have the moment.” —
Naval Ravikant

q: Normality is a paved road: It's comfortable to walk, but no flowers grow on it.
h:
“Normality is a paved road: It's comfortable to walk, but no flowers grow on it.” —
Vincent van Gogh

q: Our virtues and our failings are inseparable, like force and matter. When they separate, man is no more.
h:
“Our virtues and our failings are inseparable, like force and matter. When they separate, man is no more. ” —
Nikola Tesla

q: Youth has no age.
h:
“Youth has no age.” —
Pablo Picasso
